title: Friday Sermon 1 March 2002
description:
Continuing with the theme of the Divine attribute of al-Aziz (The Mighty) with reference to the Quranic verses that mention the said attribute - In its application to Allah, the attribute of 'The Mighty' becomes free from the concept of any force or coercion and connotes an Honourable and Mighty Being.

There is salvation in the sincere expression of La ilaha illallah [‘There is none worthy of worship save Allah’) and that it is indeed the précis of the message of Islam.  There are also healing properties in the recitation of the Surah al-Fatiha [The Opening].

(Abu Abdullah Muhammad ibn Majah.  'Sunan ibn Majah').

The Holy Prophet (peace and blessings be on him) had said that the message of Islam would reach the point where night and day meet and that there was honour for those who would submit to the Truth.*

On the concept of Paradise therein shall be a continual transformation by Allah and that Allah's capacity to bring this about was infinite.

(Hazrat Mirza Ghulam Ahmad.  2 January 1903.  'Malfuzat: iv'.  15).

The attribute of al-Aziz is further explained in the context of the Quranic perspective of the life and death of Jesus (on whom be peace) and this is further elucidated in the writings of the Promised Messiah (on whom be peace):

… their saying (of the Jews) that, ‘We have killed the Messiah, Jesus son of Mary, Messenger of God’,** while in fact they neither killed him nor crucified him; rather, this matter became dubious for them.  And as for the people who differ with regard to Jesus (namely, the Christians say that Jesus was raised alive to heaven, and the Jews say that they killed him), both of these groups are in a state of absolute  doubt.  They know nothing at all about the reality of the situation, and they do not have definitive knowledge, but are merely following their conjectures.  In other words, Jesus neither ascended to heaven as is the thinking of the Christians, nor was he killed at the hands of the Jews as is the conjecture of the Jews, but the correct situation is a third one; namely,  that he was saved [from the cross] and migrated to another country.  And the Jews themselves are not certain that they killed him.  In fact, God exalted him towards Himself, and God is Almighty and Wise.
  
THE PRAYER OF JESUS SON OF MARY, PEACE BE UPON BOTH OF THEM—He said: ‘O my God, I do not deem myself able to overcome that which I see as evil.  Nor have I acquired the virtue that I had desired.  But while others have their rewards in their hands, I do not, and my pride is in my work.  None is worse than me.  O God— who art the Most High—forgive my sins.  O God, do not make me a target for my enemies’ criticism, nor make me such as is disgraced by my friends.  And do not cause my righteousness to become a trial for me.  Do not make this world the centre of my joy or my ultimate objective.  And do not subjugate me under someone who does not have mercy on me.  O God, who art Most Merciful, in the name of Your mercy, do as I ask.  You do indeed have mercy on those who are in need of it.’

(Ahmad.  'Barāhīn -  E - Aḥmadiyya: v'.  5 vols, Anwār Ahmadiyya Machine Press, 1908.  'Ruhani Khazain: xxi'.  336, 337, 344, 345.  English: Chaudhary Muhammad Ali, Islam International Publications Ltd, 2018.  456, 464).

* Abu Abdullah Muhammad ibn Majah.  'Sunan ibn Majah'.  
** 'The Holy Quran'.  Al Nisa [The Women]: 158.

The text https://www.alislam.org/urdu/sermon/FST20020301-UR.pdf
